title: Grain Seaborne Trade Impact On The Dry Bulk Freight Market
section_path: Grain Seaborne Trade Impact On The Dry Bulk Freight Market
section_path_text: Grain Seaborne Trade Impact On The Dry Bulk Freight Market
summary:
Main
Shifts in Global Grain Trade and Freight Market Dynamics August, 2024
In our latest monthly review, we explore the ever-evolving landscape of seaborne grain trade from 2023 to 2024, leveraging insights from the Signal Ocean Platform. By tracking these shifts, we aim to uncover their effects on dry bulk freight market trends, especially within the Supramax and Panamax vessel categories. The non-renewal of the Black Sea Grain Initiative last summer has raised significant concerns regarding the volume of seaborne grain exports.

children.title: SECTION I : GRAIN FLOWS THE GLOBAL VIEW
section_path: SECTION I : GRAIN FLOWS THE GLOBAL VIEW
children.section_path_text: SECTION I : GRAIN FLOWS THE GLOBAL VIEW
children.summary: The year 2024 confirms the previous trend recorded in 2023, where Brazil appeared at the top ranking of origin countries, surpassing the percentage share of the United States with 27% instead of 18%. (Image 1) A year ago, the United States competed closely with Brazil, each having a similar percentage share of about 21%.

children.title: AN EYE ON THE UKRAIN SITUATION
section_path: AN EYE ON THE UKRAIN SITUATION
children.section_path_text: AN EYE ON THE UKRAIN SITUATION
children.summary: From Ukraine, it is interesting to see the increase in the quantity of tonnes sent this year compared to the same period a year ago. (Image 2) The improvement recorded in the flow of shipments on a quarterly basis has been significant.
